characters.physical_details: Average height and easy to overlook, with blunt-cut brown hair, ink-stained fingers, and a right-palm burn from the Grinlow coffer. By the battle he carries nosebleeds, cracked nails, and visible sleep loss.

key_relationships.martha_sorrell: Hufflepuff dorm-mate and closest early friend, later estranged after his refusal to help her
key_relationships.prue_hollis: Ravenclaw collaborator whose trust turns into intimate moral witness
key_relationships.neville_longbottom: resistance leader who wants practical loyalty and grows to distrust him
key_relationships.severus_snape: headmaster who partly understands the danger and partly surveils him
key_relationships.amycus_carrow: immediate bodily threat and enforcer of the school's cruelty
key_relationships.tallow: reluctant guide into the castle's older layers

characters.occupation_status: Hufflepuff fifth-year under Carrow rule and secret researcher of the First Art.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads English, school spell Latin, and basic runes; by chapter 10 he can parse fragments of Meridian notation.

state_transitions.change: Receives a persistent burn across his right palm when he opens the Grinlow coffer.
Ordinary wandwork becomes erratic after his first amateur amendment.
Loses Martha's friendship and his remaining good standing with the resistance.
Suffers severe magical strain: nosebleeds, cracked nails, altered magical signature, and partial bodily exhaustion from sustained First Art use.
Ends the book as secret custodian of the Hogwarts ward node rather than an ordinary student survivor.

characters.availability: Present all year; below the castle during the battle's decisive stretch.

characters.physical_details: Compact and broad-shouldered, usually with a loosening dark plait and hands marked by carrying and fighting. By spring she has split knuckles and a favoring limp.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: closest friend turned object of anger and disbelief
key_relationships.prue_hollis: uneasy ally linked by worry over Owen
key_relationships.neville_longbottom: resistance leader she trusts and follows
key_relationships.tallow: friendly kitchen contact who slips her food and rumors

characters.occupation_status: Hufflepuff fifth-year and active student resistance courier.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads English and standard Hogwarts magic texts; no First Art literacy.

state_transitions.change: Learns Owen has some unexplained defensive capacity when his amendment diverts punishment magic.
Takes major punishment after the failed corridor sabotage.
Ends her friendship with Owen after he consciously declines to intervene for her.
Enters the Battle of Hogwarts already physically compromised.

characters.availability: Present through the battle and alive afterward, but no longer emotionally available to Owen.

characters.physical_details: Tall and narrow-faced, with unruly dark curls, smudged wire-rimmed spectacles, and ink on her cuffs. She carries books like tools.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: bibliographic ally, intellectual near-intimacy, and final witness to his refusal
key_relationships.martha_sorrell: fellow student who shares concern and frustration over Owen
key_relationships.neville_longbottom: resistance contact she respects but does not fully join
key_relationships.tallow: mutual tolerance built around library and kitchen routes

characters.occupation_status: Ravenclaw sixth-year with strong archival and astronomical habits.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads English fluently; strong on catalog systems, star charts, and runic annotation; learns enough Meridian logic to follow Owen into the anchor work.

state_transitions.change: Becomes Owen's first real collaborator on the manuscript.
Learns the four-anchor structure of the failing wards.
Breaks intimate trust with Owen after he refuses a human-scale compromise.
Directly witnesses that Owen could end the battle locally and chooses not to.

characters.availability: Present through the battle and aftermath; one of the few people who knows Owen's full decision.

characters.physical_details: Solidly built, bruised often, and increasingly worn down by too little rest.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: student he wants either useful or out of the way
key_relationships.severus_snape: headmaster he opposes as part of the occupying regime

characters.occupation_status: Gryffindor student and de facto organizer of the in-school resistance.
characters.literacy_languages: Standard Hogwarts English and spell literacy; no direct access to First Art knowledge.

state_transitions.change: Rejects Owen's manuscript theory as unusable in wartime conditions.
Begins treating Owen as potentially compromised after the Snape summons.
Shifts from covert organizer to open battle commander when Harry returns.

characters.availability: Present all year and central to the battle above ground.

characters.physical_details: Sallow, black-haired, and severe, moving with controlled exhaustion.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: student under watch because of ward disturbances and unusual curiosity
key_relationships.neville_longbottom: persistent internal dissident and disciplinary problem
key_relationships.amycus_carrow: nominal ally he must manage while serving a larger covert game

characters.occupation_status: Headmaster under Voldemort's regime, outwardly complicit and inwardly playing a different war.
characters.literacy_languages: Highly literate in English, advanced magical theory, potion texts, and enough older scholarship to recognize pre-wand amendment traditions.

state_transitions.change: Confronts Owen directly about the old work without confiscating it.
Dead before dawn aftermath is fully processed by students.

characters.availability: Present through chapter 15; dead by the dawn aftermath.

characters.physical_details: Thickset and heavy-handed, quick to flush, smelling of sweat, stale drink, and borrowed authority.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: student target whose odd survivals start to irritate him
key_relationships.neville_longbottom: student enemy he cannot quite pin down
key_relationships.severus_snape: superior he resents and uses when convenient

characters.occupation_status: Death Eater Dark Arts professor and chief student-level enforcer.
characters.literacy_languages: Adequate ordinary magical literacy; no access to or understanding of the First Art.

state_transitions.change: Notices that Owen survives punishment in a way he does not understand.
Escalates public punishment after the corridor fiasco.
Removed from power by the battle's end.

characters.availability: Present as an active threat until the battle; out of power afterward.

characters.physical_details: Small even for a house-elf, soot-darkened and singe-eared, with damp working hands. Old-work spaces force him to slow.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: reluctant guide who grows wary of the boy's moral scale
key_relationships.martha_sorrell: favored student helper in kitchen errands
key_relationships.prue_hollis: tolerated scholar because she handles books and utensils carefully

characters.occupation_status: House-elf attached to kitchens, library spillover work, and old maintenance routes.
characters.literacy_languages: Can read labels, inventories, and old route marks; knows more founder-era maintenance notation than any student.

state_transitions.change: Gives Owen enough route information to reach the lost shelf-chase.
Personally leads Owen to the East Undercroft despite taboo.
Stops offering Owen unearned warmth while still protecting the house through him when necessary.

characters.availability: Present in kitchens and service routes throughout the novel; pained by the deepest First Art spaces.

timeline.story_start: Early September 1997, the first Hogwarts term under Snape after the Ministry falls to Voldemort.
timeline.estimated_span: Roughly eight months, from start-of-term arrival to dawn after 2 May 1998.

seasonal_track.season: night of 1 May 1998 through dawn on 2 May 1998

key_events.event: Owen finds the impossible footnote in a pre-1883 classroom copy of A History of Magic.
He recovers the Grinlow Leaves from the retired shelf-chase behind the north stacks.
The Room of Requirement rejects Owen's warning, but Prue notices the impossible citation.
Owen's first amendment in the greenhouse proves the First Art works and destabilizes his normal spellcasting.
A punishment scene forces Owen to use the First Art locally, leaving a ward scar.
The East Undercroft and Hogwarts anchor stone confirm the planetary ward system.
The corridor sabotage fails and Martha is punished.
The Departure Chamber shows that battle-scale magic at Hogwarts will widen the ward breach.
Harry returns, the battle begins, and Owen descends to the anchor instead of joining the fight.
Owen patches the ward edge, declines to use the combat-ending clause, and the war above ends by other hands.
key_events.timing: late night 1 May into early 2 May 1998

key_events.event: Prue witnesses the refusal and dawn reveals the social cost of Owen's hidden victory.

geography.primary_setting: Hogwarts under occupation: still recognizably a school, but bent by punishment, fear, and covert resistance.

key_locations.name: Hufflepuff cellar and common room
key_locations.details: Warm, round-ceilinged, food-scented, and close to the kitchens and their service passages.

key_locations.name: Great Hall
key_locations.details: The public stage of occupation where the Carrows humiliate students and meal ritual keeps trying to survive.

key_locations.name: Library north stacks
key_locations.details: Punitive blasting has shifted shelving here and exposed obsolete shelf numerals from before the 1894 reorganization.

key_locations.name: Retired shelf-chase and lead coffer niche
key_locations.details: Narrow hidden run behind the old Restricted Section; it holds the Grinlow coffer and is too tight for most adults.

key_locations.name: Abandoned greenhouse
key_locations.details: Cold, half-neglected space where Owen performs the first unsafe amendment because breakage seems containable.

key_locations.name: Kitchens and adjacent service runs
key_locations.details: The main quiet space, full of labor, food, heat, and elf movement; also the route into older castle infrastructure.

key_locations.name: East Undercroft
key_locations.details: Founder-era chamber beneath Hogwarts containing one planetary ward anchor stone.

key_locations.name: Departure Chamber
key_locations.details: Deeper site beneath the lake with a star-lens and records of the Meridian Commonwealth's departure.

key_locations.name: Room of Requirement
key_locations.details: Resistance planning room and the site of Owen's first public humiliation.

key_locations.name: Headmaster's office
key_locations.details: Snape's controlled territory, heavy with surveillance and formal authority.

distances: The Hufflepuff cellar sits nearest the kitchens, letting students move between them without crossing public routes.
The library north stacks are several staircases above the cellar level, but the retired shelf-chase links downward toward house-elf service space.
The East Undercroft is best reached from kitchen-side service runs once Tallow opens the route.
The Departure Chamber lies below the undercroft and nearer the lake than the castle proper.

world_rules: The story takes place during the 1997-1998 Hogwarts year under Snape, with the Carrows using lessons and detention as instruments of terror.
Ordinary Hogwarts protections still constrain movement; Apparition is not a viable way around the castle.
The First Art is not spellcasting but conditional amendment: it edits the rules under which spells, thresholds, and magical categories operate.
First Art requires inscription, prepared surfaces, or ancient architecture; it cannot be improvised as clean wand theatrics in open air.
An amendment acts by category and condition rather than by obedient target, so imprecision creates sideways and often comic collateral effects.
Every successful amendment scars local wards and destabilizes nearby conventional spellwork for a time.
Founder-era stone makes First Art easier to hold; transient spaces and moving objects resist it.
House-elves can traverse service spaces and sense old-work pressure more readily than students, but the deepest sites pain them.
The Grinlow Leaves resist cataloguing, Summoning, and full legibility; critical pages reveal themselves only after a prior amendment or at an active node.
The Meridian Commonwealth departed Earth through purpose-built transit infrastructure after constructing masking wards around the planet.
Large-scale magical violence makes those masking wards brighter and more noticeable to the unnamed predatory attention outside them.

power_structure.over: Severus Snape and Hogwarts as institution
power_structure.mechanism: Political conquest backed by murder and terror.

power_structure.over: Students and remaining staff at Hogwarts
power_structure.mechanism: Formal headmaster authority under the occupying regime, enforced through fear and delegated violence.

power_structure.over: Students in class, detention, and corridor discipline
power_structure.mechanism: Direct bodily violence, Dark Arts instruction, and permission from the regime.
Earned trust, repeated courage, and willingness to take punishment first.

power_structure.over: Kitchen-side service knowledge and certain hidden routes
power_structure.mechanism: Institutional memory, labor access, and house-elf authority inside maintenance space.

power_structure.over: The Hogwarts anchor node by the battle chapters
power_structure.mechanism: Partial First Art competence acquired through obsession rather than sanction.

objects.name: The Grinlow Leaves
objects.owner: Owen Phipps after taking it from the shelf-chase
objects.origin: Founder-era manuscript hidden in a lead coffer behind the retired Restricted Section.
objects.status: Recovered in chapter 2, studied through the year, and rehidden or reabsorbed into concealment in chapter 18.

objects.name: Lead coffer from North Stacks C-13
objects.owner: Originally part of Hogwarts' old library concealment architecture
objects.origin: Placed in the retired shelf-chase to keep the Grinlow Leaves off catalog and out of Summoning range.
objects.status: Forced open by Owen in chapter 2 and left damaged in the hidden niche.

objects.name: Owen's ash-linen notebook
objects.origin: Ordinary school supply turned into a private record of amendments, tables, and routes.
objects.status: Accumulates singes, corrections, and dangerous notes; remains with Owen by the end.

objects.name: Hogwarts anchor stone
objects.owner: No personal owner; part of the Meridian ward system embedded in the castle
objects.origin: Founder-era installation tied to the planetary masking wards.
objects.status: Discovered in chapter 7, damaged but functioning, and partially repaired in chapter 16.

objects.name: Departure Chamber star-lens
objects.owner: No personal owner; fixed instrument of the older system
objects.origin: Meridian infrastructure beneath the lake used to read ward conditions beyond Earth.
objects.status: Activated in chapter 13; usable enough to reveal the breach threat but not fully repaired.

objects.name: Latent combat clause in Hogwarts' old protections
objects.owner: Embedded in the anchor's available amendments rather than portable
objects.origin: Residual founder-era local condition that can suspend hostile wandwork within the castle if correctly invoked.
objects.status: Discovered in chapter 16 and intentionally left unused during the battle.

knowledge_state.knows: The footnote to North Stacks C-13 exists in a pre-1883 copy and points to a real hidden location.
The Grinlow Leaves describe the First Art and imply the Meridian Commonwealth left Earth behind wards.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: All other characters until Prue is brought in.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen quoted a citation that cannot exist in any modern edition of A History of Magic.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Neville, Martha, Snape, and the wider resistance.

knowledge_state.knows: His first amendment changed a category rather than a single target and destabilized his ordinary spellcasting.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone except indirect signs visible to Prue later.

knowledge_state.knows: The manuscript can be surfaced through astronomy overlays and is not merely a frightened fantasy.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Martha, Neville, Snape, and the Carrows.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen has some unexplained way of diverting or altering punishment magic.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Neville and Amycus both lack the cause.

knowledge_state.knows: Hogwarts contains a ward anchor in the East Undercroft tied to a planetary masking system.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: All other students and the Carrows.

knowledge_state.knows: There are four failing anchors globally, with Hogwarts under unusual stress because of repeated violence.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Martha, Neville, Snape, and the Carrows.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen is involved with old amendment magic serious enough to scar the castle wards.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Neville and most students; the Carrows know only that Owen is strange.

knowledge_state.knows: A battle at Hogwarts will widen the ward breach and demands immediate anchor repair.
The anchor contains a local clause that could suspend hostile wandwork across Hogwarts during the battle.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: All characters until Prue reaches the chamber.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen consciously refuses to use the local combat-ending clause while the battle is still undecided.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Most survivors, including Martha and Neville, unless Prue later speaks.

environmental_constants: The castle is cold in a way that finds wrists, ankles, and knees even when fires are lit.
Occupied Hogwarts smells of soot, old paper, damp wool, and institutional food trying to be comforting.
The kitchens are warmer, wetter, and louder than the upper school, grounding the book's quiet scenes in labor and food.
The library's damaged north side keeps a grit of dust and splintered wood after the blasting that exposed the old shelf numerals.
First Art sites feel wrong in the body before they are explained: tooth-pressure, hot stone, ringing ears, nausea, thickened air.
The undercroft is damp and mineral, with sounds traveling through stone as thuds rather than clear voices.
Ordinary school textures remain beside terror: bread crust, ink, homework, and greenhouse soil.
During the battle, smoke and stone dust infiltrate even subterranean scenes.
